sections.paragraph: ᾅδης, ου, ὁ [proper name; in Homer Ἀΐδης ‘Hades’ is god of the underworld/netherworld, which is frequently associated with his name as ‘house/abode of Hades’; in later Gk. the focus is frequently on Hades as locale, and in most Israelite circles the Greek term would be understood in the sense of the Heb. ‘sheol’] ‘abode of the dead’, Deathplace, Netherworld, Hades Ac 2:27, 31; as opposed to heaven Mt 11:23; with subdivisions for torment and blessing (approaching the standard Hellenic perception) Lk 16:23. In Rv 6:8 the netherworld is perceived as a functioning entity; cp. the extraordinary imagery 20:14.
